title: next_diversifier: deferred transaction allows concurrent callers to return same index
description: next_diversifier uses pool.begin() which issues a deferred SQLite transaction. Two concurrent callers can both read the same diversifier_idx value before either commits, then both return the same index and both write next=current+1. The result is two addresses allocated to the same diversifier index — payments sent to the duplicate address are received (the wallet sees two invoices for the same address) or one invoice silently shadows the other in get_invoice_by_address. alloc_diversifier_and_create_invoice correctly uses BEGIN IMMEDIATE, but next_diversifier standalone is still racy. It is called directly by send_payment in wallet/src/payment.rs.
